mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects accelerating consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth fails to positively correlate with subsequent 20-day SPX returns, producing a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, further increases in the policy rate raise discount rates and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over tightening periods is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal a stagflationary contraction where labor market weakness coincides with restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining PAYEMS and rising FEDFUNDS fails to underperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return by at least 2 annualized percentage points over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In periods when the unemployment rate is below 4.0%, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-constrained overheating that prompts expected monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with supply overheating
failure_condition: During UNRATE < 4.0% regimes, positive INDPRO surprises are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, refuting the tightening-fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining HOUST is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months, or the Rank IC between HOUST changes and forward returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer income expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX posts positive 20-day returns in over 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.2pp over 3 months, or the conditional Sharpe is not statistically below zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) confirm a reactive monetary tightening into an inflationary shock, which compresses real earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of joint-signal months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate top-line growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or the Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window falls below 0.03.
